title: sambucus australasica
intro: Sambucus australasica, also known as the native elderberry, is a plant species native to Australia and New Zealand, belonging to the Sambucus genus of flowering plants. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its flowers, leaves, and berries exhibiting medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and fever. The medicinal properties of Sambucus australasica are attributed to its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing the severity and duration of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Sambucus australasica, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, can be made from the plant's flowers, leaves, and berries, and are often used in natural remedies for their therapeutic effects.
